texts.full_text: season.So let'sfocus on the good:Cody Glenn. People pooh-poohed his conversion to linebacker, buthe was solidmostof theday. Not perfect, by any means. But he made plays all day,and that's something we didn't see much of last season ondefense.Joe Ganz. Well, at least the first three quarters, a very solid performance running and passing. He's the first Husker quarterbacktohave four passinggames, whichis impressive considering he's only started four games.Nate Swift.Yeah, he's not a speed burThe article describesNebraska'sopeninggame against Western Michigan on August 30,2014,a season markedby challenges for theCornhuskers.GameOverview: - Outcome: The Huskersexperienced a "mistake-filled"game and ultimately lost showing some early promise. Key Performances & Analysis: 1. Transitioning from safety tol inebacker,Glenn stood out with solid performance,making key plays throughout thegame. 2. Despitehaving a strong first three quarterswithimpressive stats(307 yards passingand four touchdowns),Ganz's second half mistakes(including two interceptions) led to turnovers that affectedNebraska’s chances of winning. His game marked significantachievement as he became NU'sfirst QB with multiple 4-touchdown games inasingle outing. - The team exhibited recurring errors, particularlyinthe latterhalfofthe gamewhich cost them opportunities to maintain control. 3.: His early impact was promising but cut short due tolater injury. Maintaining his healthwould have bolstered Nebraska'soffense against tougher upcoming opponents likeFlorida State and Michigan. texts.full_text: (this appears in Milne). Then we know what this ideal is - it is spanned by the wedge product of Plücker coordinates. In particular, the coordinate ring of the image is $\bigwedge^{k+1}V / I$, where $I$ is the ideal spanned by the image in $R$ and the universal minors. texts.full_text: not included within this filing process Hearing on the objection was held May 11, 1999, neither side sought briefing time and the following Order is entered. In his Schedules, Debtor lists his residence as having a value of $86,000 and a joint mortgage of $18,545.28. He seeks to exempt as entireties property the entire $67,454.72 in equity in the house. In addition, the debtor lists exemptions for three vehicles as entireties property: a $1,000 exemption in a 1978 LTD which he values at $1,000; a $2,000 exemption

texts.full_text: districts. In response to the USDOJ's administrative determination, the General Assembly convened in April 1982 and enacted a revised redistricting plan for the House, creating four African-American single-member districts and one African-American two-member district. The House Plan divided twenty-four counties. Act of February 11, 1982, ch. 4, 1981 N.C. Sess. Laws (1st Extra Sess. 1982) 6; Act of April 27, 1982, ch. 1, 1981 *388 N.C. Sess. Laws (2d Extra Sess.1982) 15. On 30 April 1982, the USDOJ precleared the House redistricting plan. Similarly, the General Assembly enacted a revised redistricting plan for the Senate, which the USDOJ also precleared, that divided eight counties and created two African-American single-member districts.
